column1: #   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   classes and the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 ISMS LinkML schema. OCSF Compliance
#   and policy objects align with ISO 27001 security control and policy
#   concepts. Vulnerability and security findings map to the ISO 27001 risk
#   and audit-finding lifecycle. Remediation maps to risk treatment planning.

column8: OCSF Compliance records the assessment state of a security control against a standard; ISO 27001 SecurityControl represents an Annex A control with applicability and implementation status.
A ComplianceFinding event signals that a policy or control check produced a result; ISO 27001 AuditFinding is the structured record of such a result in an ISMS internal audit.
A vulnerability finding represents a realised or potential risk to information assets; ISO 27001 Risk captures the likelihood/consequence assessment of such threats.
A generic security finding indicates a potential risk; the ISO 27001 Risk entity is the closest structural equivalent in the ISMS lifecycle.
OCSF Remediation describes recommended fix actions; ISO 27001 RiskTreatmentPlan formalises the selected controls and owner accountabilities for treating a risk.
OCSF Policy captures the applicable security policy at event time; ISO 27001 InformationSecurityPolicy is the top-level ISMS policy document — close but OCSF policy is more operational.
CIS Critical Security Controls are widely mapped to ISO 27001 Annex A controls; both model prioritised technical and organisational safeguards.
